revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nagoya, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}} | image = Shiroyama6.JPG | caption = Shiroyama Hachimangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|03|N|136|57|35|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = [[Nagoya]], central [[Japan]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}}, also known as ''Shiroyama Hakusan'', is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Nagoya]], central [[Japan]].
== History == The shrine is located on the premises of the ruined [[Suemori Castle]]. The castle itself dates back to the 16th century. The shrine hosts night-time festivals (''[[matsuri]]'') in both July and October, featuring traditional Japanese music and dance performances.
An unusual feature is a sacred Marital Tree, whose trunk split into two parts and grew back together at a later time. This tree is worshipped as a symbol of happy marriage and restoration of relationships.
Access by public transport is [[Motoyama Station (Aichi)|Motoyama Station]] on the [[Higashiyama Line]] and then a five minutes walk uphill to the north.
== External links == * [http://www.shiroyama.or.jp/ Homepage of Shiroyama Hachiman Shrine]
{{Shinto shrine}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiroyama Hachimangu}} [[Category:Chikusa-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nagoya, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}} | image = Shiroyama6.JPG | caption = Shiroyama Hachimangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|03|N|136|57|35|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = [[Nagoya]], central [[Japan]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}}, also known as ''Shiroyama Hakusan'', is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Nagoya]], central [[Japan]].
== History == The shrine is located on the premises of the ruined [[Suemori Castle]]. The castle itself dates back to the 16th century. The shrine hosts night-time festivals (''[[matsuri]]'') in both July and October, featuring traditional Japanese music and dance performances.
An unusual feature is a sacred Marital Tree, whose trunk split into two parts and grew back together at a later time. This tree is worshipped as a symbol of happy marriage and restoration of relationships.
Access by public transport is [[Motoyama Station (Aichi)|Motoyama Station]] on the [[Higashiyama Line]] and then a five minutes walk uphill to the north.
== External links == * [http://www.shiroyama.or.jp/ Homepage of Shiroyama Hachiman Shrine]
{{Shinto shrine}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nagoya, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}} | image = Shiroyama6.JPG | caption = Shiroyama Hachimangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|03|N|136|57|35|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = [[Nagoya]], central [[Japan]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}}, also known as ''Shiroyama Hakusan'', is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Nagoya]], central [[Japan]].
== History == The shrine is located on the premises of the ruined [[Suemori Castle]]. The castle itself dates back to the 16th century. The shrine hosts night-time festivals (''[[matsuri]]'') in both July and October, featuring traditional Japanese music and dance performances.
An unusual feature is a sacred Marital Tree, whose trunk split into two parts and grew back together at a later time. This tree is worshipped as a symbol of happy marriage and restoration of relationships.
Access by public transport is [[Motoyama Station (Aichi)|Motoyama Station]] on the [[Higashiyama Line]] and then a five minutes walk uphill to the north.
== External links == * [http://www.shiroyama.or.jp/ Homepage of Shiroyama Hachiman Shrine]
{{Shinto shrine}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiroyama Hachimangu}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:Chikusa-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[en:Shiroyama Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Shiroyama Hachimangū]]{{Shinto-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nagoya, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}} | image = Shiroyama6.JPG | caption = Shiroyama Hachimangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|03|N|136|57|35|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = [[Nagoya]], central [[Japan]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}}, also known as ''Shiroyama Hakusan'', is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Nagoya]], central [[Japan]].
== History == The shrine is located on the premises of the ruined [[Suemori Castle]]. The castle itself dates back to the 16th century. The shrine hosts night-time festivals (''[[matsuri]]'') in both July and October, featuring traditional Japanese music and dance performances.
An unusual feature is a sacred Marital Tree, whose trunk split into two parts and grew back together at a later time. This tree is worshipped as a symbol of happy marriage and restoration of relationships.
Access by public transport is [[Motoyama Station (Aichi)|Motoyama Station]] on the [[Higashiyama Line]] and then a five minutes walk uphill to the north.
== External links == * [http://www.shiroyama.or.jp/ Homepage of Shiroyama Hachiman Shrine]
{{Shinto shrine}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Shiroyama Hachimangū]][[fr:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:城山八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3482375]] Wikidata name: Shiroyama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nagoya, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}} | image = Shiroyama6.JPG | caption = Shiroyama Hachimangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|03|N|136|57|35|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], central [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}}, also known as ''Shiroyama Hakusan'', is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], central [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
== History == The shrine is located on the premises of the ruined [[:en:Suemori Castle|Suemori Castle]]. The castle itself dates back to the 16th century. The shrine hosts night-time festivals (''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'') in both July and October, featuring traditional Japanese music and dance performances.
An unusual feature is a sacred Marital Tree, whose trunk split into two parts and grew back together at a later time. This tree is worshipped as a symbol of happy marriage and restoration of relationships.
Access by public transport is [[:en:Motoyama Station (Aichi)|Motoyama Station]] on the [[:en:Higashiyama Line|Higashiyama Line]] and then a five minutes walk uphill to the north.
== External links == * [http://www.shiroyama.or.jp/ Homepage of Shiroyama Hachiman Shrine] <!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including-->{{Shinto-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Chikusa-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]]
}}
[[en:Shiroyama Hachimangū]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Shiroyama Hachimangū]][[fr:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:城山八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3482375]] Wikidata name: Shiroyama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nagoya, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}} | image = Shiroyama6.JPG | caption = Shiroyama Hachimangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|03|N|136|57|35|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], central [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}}, also known as ''Shiroyama Hakusan'', is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], central [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
== History == The shrine is located on the premises of the ruined [[:en:Suemori Castle|Suemori Castle]]. The castle itself dates back to the 16th century. The shrine hosts night-time festivals (''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'') in both July and October, featuring traditional Japanese music and dance performances.
An unusual feature is a sacred Marital Tree, whose trunk split into two parts and grew back together at a later time. This tree is worshipped as a symbol of happy marriage and restoration of relationships.
Access by public transport is [[:en:Motoyama Station (Aichi)|Motoyama Station]] on the [[:en:Higashiyama Line|Higashiyama Line]] and then a five minutes walk uphill to the north.
== External links == * [http://www.shiroyama.or.jp/ Homepage of Shiroyama Hachiman Shrine] <!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including-->{{Shinto-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Shiroyama Hachimangū]][[fr:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:城山八幡宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Shiroyama Hachimangū]][[fr:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:城山八幡宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Shiroyama Hachimangū]][[fr:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:城山八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3482375]] Wikidata name: Shiroyama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nagoya, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}} | image = Shiroyama6.JPG | caption = Shiroyama Hachimangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|03|N|136|57|35|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], central [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}}, also known as ''Shiroyama Hakusan'', is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], central [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
== History == The shrine is located on the premises of the ruined [[:en:Suemori Castle|Suemori Castle]]. The castle itself dates back to the 16th century. The shrine hosts night-time festivals (''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'') in both July and October, featuring traditional Japanese music and dance performances.
An unusual feature is a sacred Marital Tree, whose trunk split into two parts and grew back together at a later time. This tree is worshipped as a symbol of happy marriage and restoration of relationships.
Access by public transport is [[:en:Motoyama Station (Aichi)|Motoyama Station]] on the [[:en:Higashiyama Line|Higashiyama Line]] and then a five minutes walk uphill to the north.
== External links == * [http://www.shiroyama.or.jp/ Homepage of Shiroyama Hachiman Shrine] <!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including-->{{Shinto-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Shiroyama Hachimangū]][[fr:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:城山八幡宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Shiroyama Hachimangū]][[fr:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:城山八幡宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Shiroyama Hachimangū]][[fr:Shiroyama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:城山八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3482375]] Wikidata name: Shiroyama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nagoya, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}} | image = Shiroyama6.JPG | caption = Shiroyama Hachimangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|03|N|136|57|35|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}}, central {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}}, also known as ''Shiroyama Hakusan'', is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}}, central {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.
== History == The shrine is located on the premises of the ruined {{ill|Suemori Castle|en|Suemori Castle|WD=Q846681|lt=Suemori Castle}}. The castle itself dates back to the 16th century. The shrine hosts night-time festivals (''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'') in both July and October, featuring traditional Japanese music and dance performances.
An unusual feature is a sacred Marital Tree, whose trunk split into two parts and grew back together at a later time. This tree is worshipped as a symbol of happy marriage and restoration of relationships.
Access by public transport is [[:en:Motoyama Station (Aichi)|Motoyama Station]] on the {{ill|Higashiyama Line|en|Higashiyama Line|WD=Q1132799|lt=Higashiyama Line}} and then a five minutes walk uphill to the north.
== External links == * [http://www.shiroyama.or.jp/ Homepage of Shiroyama Hachiman Shrine] <!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including-->{{Shinto-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nagoya, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}} | image = Shiroyama6.JPG | caption = Shiroyama Hachimangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|03|N|136|57|35|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, central {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shiroyama Hachimangū'''|城山八幡宮}}, also known as ''Shiroyama Hakusan'', is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, central {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.
== History == The shrine is located on the premises of the ruined {{ill|Suemori Castle|en|Suemori Castle|WD=Q846681|lt=Suemori Castle|1=Suemori Castle}}. The castle itself dates back to the 16th century. The shrine hosts night-time festivals (''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'') in both July and October, featuring traditional Japanese music and dance performances.
An unusual feature is a sacred Marital Tree, whose trunk split into two parts and grew back together at a later time. This tree is worshipped as a symbol of happy marriage and restoration of relationships.
Access by public transport is [[:en:Motoyama Station (Aichi)|Motoyama Station]] on the {{ill|Higashiyama Line|en|Higashiyama Line|WD=Q1132799|lt=Higashiyama Line|1=Higashiyama Line}} and then a five minutes walk uphill to the north.
